extraction.one_line_summary: ANDERSON Søren Kragh won from breakaway after Bora drove tempo on early climbs trying to drop Bennett before intermediate sprint, sacrificing sprint points for Bennett's green jersey defense.
tactical_patterns: Bora entire team rode tempo on Cat-4 climb 3 miles before sprint to distance Bennett, prioritizing attrition over sprint positioning.
Kung made explosive acceleration to bridge gap solo using time trial power, revealing breakaway selection depends on TT specialists' bridging ability.
Bennett's team fought hard on 6-mile climb mid-stage to defend minimal green jersey lead, exposing sprinter teams lack climbing depth.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: required full team support just to limit losses on what's supposedly a 'flat' stage with extended climbs
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Bora's tactical effort on climbs gained only 3-4 points despite massive team expenditure, revealing diminishing returns strategy
rider_signals.KUNG Stefan: positive: used TT power to bridge solo with explosive acceleration, showing time trialists can force breakaway selection
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: mechanical at critical moment nearly cost GC time, continuing pattern of ill-timed setbacks
